pi_items.source: Adobe Commerce documentation and GitHub references confirm this is the real configuration path for 'Allow Gift Messages on Order Level' setting in Magento 2. Verified via multiple authoritative sources including Adobe Commerce official documentation.
setup_magento.sh line 281: Product explicitly created via REST API with sku='LAPTOP-001', name='Business Laptop Pro', price=999.99, category='Electronics', qty=50

Task requirement specifies this email for guest checkout. Not pre-created in setup_magento.sh (only creates john.doe@example.com, jane.smith@example.com, mike.wilson@example.com). This email is created by the agent during task execution as a guest order.
Task description line 6: 'add a Gift Message for the order with To: "Alice", From: "Bob"'. Verifier.py line 33 confirms expected_sender='Bob'
Task description line 6: 'add a Gift Message for the order with To: "Alice", From: "Bob"'. Verifier.py line 34 confirms expected_recipient='Alice'
Task description line 6: 'Message: "Happy Birthday! Enjoy the new laptop."' contains both required keywords. Verifier.py line 35 confirms keywords=['Happy Birthday', 'Enjoy the new laptop']

privileged_info_summary: The gift message workflow task is built on synthetic test data generated by setup scripts. The target product 'Business Laptop Pro' with SKU 'LAPTOP-001' is explicitly seeded in the Magento environment via REST API with a price of $999.99. The task requires enabling the Magento configuration setting 'sales/gift_options/allow_order' (which controls 'Allow Gift Messages on Order Level' functionality) and placing a guest order with email 'test.gift@example.com' that includes a gift message from 'Bob' to 'Alice' containing the text 'Happy Birthday! Enjoy the new laptop.' The data is entirely synthetic, created during Magento environment setup.
